hook.text: The Sahara Desert contains some of the straightest borders on Earth.

buildup.text: Colonial powers drew lines through sand where no geographic feature existed to follow.

discovery.text: Nomadic peoples like the Tuareg have crossed these invisible lines for millennia.

twist.text: Some Saharan borders are so remote that no fence, wall, or marker exists for hundreds of km.

climax.text: In the desert, the border exists on paper but vanishes in the sand.

punchline.text: You can cross a border without knowing it exists.

quiz.question: Why are many Saharan borders straight lines?
options: No natural features existed to follow
